body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: ToolLoop advisor redesign
body.description:
**What changed:** ToolLoop redesigned with round-based iteration (5 per round, 2 rounds max) and advisor meta-call between rounds with 6 end states.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Tickets that previously needed 8-12 iterations to complete now hard-ESCALATE at the round-2 boundary — silent regression in DONE rate
- Advisor REPROMPT rewrites may drift away from original ticket intent, producing work that doesn't match what was requested
- Advisor call adds latency and an extra inference cost per ticket — total cost per DONE may rise even if per-iteration cost drops
- CONFUSED signal flood if the advisor's own context is insufficient to summarize 5 complex tool results
- Context reset may drop information the second round actually needed (e.g. a file read result from round 1)

**Signals to watch:** DONE% across first 20 post-ship tickets vs. pre-ship baseline (both escalated); avg cost_usd per DONE in MINION_RESULT channel; advisor_signal distribution; any CONFUSED rate > 10%

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15 or after 20 tickets through the new ToolLoop, whichever comes first.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** No automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed
